warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
The Fukui reactivity analysis for furan was performed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) at the tier `standard` level (B3LYP/def2-TZVP functional with density fitting in PySCF), in the gas phase, with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1. The geometry was sourced from the user-provided `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz` file and used as-is for this single-point calculation.

The Fukui indices, which indicate local reactivity, are reported for each atom:

| Atom Index (0-based) | Atom Symbol | Fukui f+ (electrophilic) | Fukui f- (nucleophilic) | Fukui f0 (radical) | Dual Descriptor |
| :------------------- | :---------- | :----------------------- | :---------------------- | :----------------- | :-------------- |
| 0                    | O           | -1.33162                 | 0.08382                 | -0.62390           | -1.41544        |
| 1                    | C           | 1.22869                  | 0.08910                 | 0.65889            | 1.13958         |
| 2                    | C           | 1.22703                  | 0.08930                 | 0.65817            | 1.13772         |
| 3                    | C           | 1.92331                  | 0.16153                 | 1.04242            | 1.76178         |
| 4                    | C           | 1.92429                  | 0.16164                 | 1.04297            | 1.76266         |
| 5                    | H           | -1.21906                 | 0.10248                 | -0.55829           | -1.32155        |
| 6                    | H           | -1.21911                 | 0.10251                 | -0.55830           | -1.32162        |
| 7                    | H           | -0.76678                 | 0.10482                 | -0.33098           | -0.87160        |
| 8                    | H           | -0.76674                 | 0.10479                 | -0.33097           | -0.87153        |

The most electrophilic site is Carbon (C) at index 4 (1-based label 5) with a Fukui f+ value of 1.9242931.
The most nucleophilic site is also Carbon (C) at index 4 (1-based label 5) with a Fukui f- value of 0.1616371.
The sum of f+ and f- values both equal 1.0000, which indicates the N±1 states converged correctly.

The calculation successfully completed and is trustworthy.
